title: Model the whole lap snapshot, not only the focal driver row
summary: Pit decisions are multi-agent and depend on rival states, not just one car’s tyre age.
rationale: Pit decisions are multi-agent and depend on rival states, not just one car’s tyre age. Recent F1 strategy papers explicitly model pit-stop decisions as interacting agents, and set models are designed for permutation-invariant group context.
implementation_hint: Build per-`(Race_Year, LapNumber)` snapshots of all drivers and encode them with either a small set model or simpler snapshot aggregates. Useful features: counts of rivals on older tyres, compound mix ahead/behind, same-stint congestion, and lap-level pit intensity. Blend the snapshot expert with the existing row-wise tree model.
expected_effect: Adds competitive context during synchronized pit windows and should improve ranking on ambiguous midfield scenarios.
risk: Heavier engineering and stronger overfitting risk because there are few races relative to rows; all snapshot features must be generated OOF by race group.
